volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 1091 - Section 1.1261)

sections.label: Section 1091
sections.original: ἁνήρ , ἄναξ , βέβηκε δεινὰ θεσπίσας ·
sections.translation: The man is gone , my king , leaving dire prophecies behind . And for all the time that I have had this hair on my head , now white , once dark , I know that he has never been a false prophet to our city .

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: ἔγνωκα καὐτὸς καὶ ταράσσομαι φρένας .
sections.translation: I , too , know it well , and my mind is troubled . To yield is terrible , but , to resist , to strike my pride with ruin — this , too , inspires terror .

sections.label: Section 1098
sections.original: εὐβουλίας δεῖ , παῖ Μενοικέως , λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: The moment , Creon , requires that you reason wisely .

sections.label: Section 1099
sections.original: τί δῆτα χρὴ δρᾶν ; φράζε . πείσομαι δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: What should I do , then ? Speak , and I will obey .

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: ἐλθὼν κόρην μὲν ἐκ κατώρυχος στέγης
sections.translation: Go and free the girl from her hollowed chamber . Then raise a tomb for the unburied dead .

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἐπαινεῖς καὶ δοκεῖς παρεικαθεῖν ;
sections.translation: And you recommend this ? You think that I should yield ?

sections.label: Section 1103
sections.original: ὅσον γʼ , ἄναξ , τάχιστα · συντέμνουσι γὰρ
sections.translation: Yes , my king , and with all possible speed . For harms sent from the gods swiftly cut short the follies of men .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: οἴμοι · μόλις μέν , καρδίας δʼ ἐξίσταμαι
sections.translation: Ah , it is a struggle , but I depart from my heart ’ s resolve and obey . We must not wage vain wars with necessity .

sections.label: Section 1107
sections.original: δρᾶ νυν τάδʼ ἐλθὼν μηδʼ ἐπʼ ἄλλοισιν τρέπε .
sections.translation: Go , do these things and do not leave their performance to others .

sections.label: Section 1108
sections.original: ὧδʼ ὡς ἔχω στείχοιμʼ ἄν · ἴτʼ ἴτʼ ὀπάονες ,
sections.translation: Right away I will go . Go , go , my servants , each and all of you ! Take axes in your hands ,

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: ὁρμᾶσθʼ ἑλόντες εἰς ἐπόψιον τόπον .
sections.translation: and hurry to that place there in view ! But since my judgment has taken this turn , I will be there to set her free , as I myself confined her . I am held by the fear that it is best to keep the established laws to life ’ s very end .

sections.label: Section 1.1115
sections.original: πολυώνυμε , Καδμείας νύμφας ἄγαλμα
sections.translation: God of many names , glory of the Cadmeian bride and offspring of loud-thundering Zeus , you who watch over far-famed Italy and reign

sections.label: Section 1.1120
sections.original: Δῃοῦς ἐν κόλποις , Βακχεῦ Βακχᾶν
sections.translation: in the valleys of Eleusinian Deo where all find welcome ! O Bacchus , denizen of Thebes , the mother-city of your Bacchants , dweller by the wet stream of Ismenus on the soil

sections.label: Section 1.1125
sections.original: Ἰσμηνοῦ ῥείθρων ἀγρίου τʼ ἐπὶ σπορᾷ δράκοντος .
sections.translation: of the sowing of the savage dragon ’ s teeth !

sections.label: Section 1.1126
sections.original: σὲ δʼ ὑπὲρ διλόφου πέτρας στέροψ ὄπωπε
sections.translation: The smoky glare of torches sees you above the cliffs of the twin peaks , where the Corycian nymphs move inspired by your godhead ,

sections.label: Section 1.1130
sections.original: Κασταλίας τε νᾶμα .
sections.translation: and Castalia ’ s stream sees you , too . The ivy-mantled slopes of Nysa ’ s hills and the shore green with many-clustered vines send you , when accompanied by the cries of your divine words ,

sections.label: Section 1.1135
sections.original: εὐαζόντων Θηβαΐας ἐπισκοποῦντʼ ἀγυιάς ·
sections.translation: you visit the avenues of Thebes .

sections.label: Section 2.1137
sections.original: τὰν ἐκ πᾶσαι τιμᾷς ὑπερτάταν πόλεων
sections.translation: Thebes of all cities you hold foremost in honor , together with your lightning-struck mother .

sections.label: Section 2.1140
sections.original: καὶ νῦν , ὡς βιαίας ἔχεται
sections.translation: And now when the whole city is held subject to a violent plague , come , we ask , with purifying feet over steep Parnassus ,

sections.label: Section 2.1145
sections.original: ἢ στονόεντα πορθμόν .
sections.translation: or over the groaning straits !

sections.label: Section 2.1146
sections.original: ἰὼ πῦρ πνειόντων χοράγʼ ἄστρων , νυχίων
sections.translation: O Leader of the chorus of the stars whose breath is fire , overseer of the chants in the night , son begotten of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 2.1150
sections.original: ὦναξ , σαῖς ἅμα περιπόλοις
sections.translation: appear , my king , with your attendant Thyiads , who in night-long frenzy dance and sing you as Iacchus the Giver !

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: Κάδμου πάροικοι καὶ δόμων Ἀμφίονος ,
sections.translation: Neighbors of the house of Cadmus and of Amphion , there is no station of human life that I would ever praise or blame as being settled . Fortune sets upright and Fortune sinks the lucky and unlucky from day to day ,

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: καὶ μάντις οὐδεὶς τῶν καθεστώτων βροτοῖς .
sections.translation: and no one can prophesy to men concerning the order that has just been established . For Creon , as I saw it , was once blest : he had saved this land of Cadmus from its enemies ; and having won sole and total dominion in the land , he guided it on a straight course and flourished in his noble crop of children .

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἀφεῖται πάντα . τὰς γὰρ ἡδονὰς
sections.translation: And now all this has been lost . When a man has forfeited his pleasures , I do not reckon his existence as life , but consider him just a breathing corpse . Heap up riches in your house , if you wish ! Live with a tyrant ’ s pomp ! But if there is no joy

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: τούτων τὸ χαίρειν , τἄλλʼ ἐγὼ καπνοῦ σκιᾶς
sections.translation: along with all of that , I would not pay even the shadow of smoke for all the rest , compared with joy .

sections.label: Section 1172
sections.original: τί δʼ αὖ τόδʼ ἄχθος βασιλέων ἥκεις φέρων ;
sections.translation: What is this new grief for our princes that you have come to report ?

sections.label: Section 1173
sections.original: τεθνᾶσιν . οἱ δὲ ζῶντες αἴτιοι θανεῖν .
sections.translation: They are dead , and the living are guilty of the deaths .

sections.label: Section 1174
sections.original: καὶ τίς φονεύει ; τίς δʼ ὁ κείμενος ; λέγε .
sections.translation: Who is the murderer ? Who the murdered ? Tell us .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: Αἵμων ὄλωλεν · αὐτόχειρ δʼ αἱμάσσεται .
sections.translation: Haemon is dead — his blood was shed by no strange hand .

sections.label: Section 1176
sections.original: πότερα πατρῴας ἢ πρὸς οἰκείας χερός ;
sections.translation: Was it his father ’ s , or his own ?

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: αὐτὸς πρὸς αὑτοῦ , πατρὶ μηνίσας φόνου .
sections.translation: He did it by his own , enraged with his father for the murder .

sections.label: Section 1178
sections.original: ὦ μάντι , τοὔπος ὡς ἄρʼ ὀρθὸν ἤνυσας .
sections.translation: Ah , prophet , how true , then , you have proved your word !

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: ὡς ὧδʼ ἐχόντων τἄλλα βουλεύειν πάρα .
sections.translation: Knowing that these things are so , you must consider the rest .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁρῶ τάλαιναν Εὐρυδίκην ὁμοῦ
sections.translation: Wait , I see the unhappy Eurydice , Creon ’ s wife , nearby . She comes from the house either knowing of her son , or merely by chance .

sections.label: Section 1183
sections.original: ὦ πάντες ἀστοί , τῶν λόγων ἐπῃσθόμην
sections.translation: People of Thebes , I heard your words as I was on my way to the gates to address divine Pallas with my prayers .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: ὅπως ἱκοίμην εὐγμάτων προσήγορος .
sections.translation: At one and the same time I was loosening the bolts of the gate to open it , and the sound of a blow to our house struck my ear . In terror I sank back into the arms of my handmaids , and my senses fled .

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὅστις ἦν ὁ μῦθος αὖθις εἴπατε ·
sections.translation: But repeat what your news was , for I shall hear it with ears that are no strangers to sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original: ἐγώ , φίλη δέσποινα , καὶ παρὼν ἐρῶ
sections.translation: Dear mistress , I will tell what I witnessed and leave no word of the truth unspoken . For what good would it do that should I soothe you with words in which I must later be found false ?

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: ψεῦσται φανούμεθʼ ; ὀρθὸν ἁλήθειʼ ἀεί .
sections.translation: The truth is always best . I attended your husband as his guide to the furthest part of the plain , where unpitied the body of Polyneices , torn by dogs , still lay . After we had prayed to the goddess of the roads

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: Πλούτωνά τʼ ὀργὰς εὐμενεῖς κατασχεθεῖν
sections.translation: and to Pluto to restrain their anger in mercy , we washed him with pure washing , and with freshly-plucked boughs we burned what remains there were . Lastly we heaped a high-mounded tomb of his native earth . Afterwards we turned away to enter the maiden ’ s stoney-bedded

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: νυμφεῖον Ἅιδου κοῖλον εἰσεβαίνομεν .
sections.translation: bridal chamber , the caverned mansion of Hades ’ bride . From a distance , one of us servants heard a voice of loud wailing near that bride ’ s unwept bed and came to tell our master Creon . And as the King moved closer and closer , obscure signs rising from a bitter cry surrounded him —

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: ἕρποντι μᾶλλον ἆσσον , οἰμώξας δʼ ἔπος
sections.translation: he groaned and said in bitter lament , Ah , misery , am I now the prophet of evil ? Am I going on the path most lined with grief of all that I have walked before ? My son ’ s voice greets me . Go , my servants ,

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: ἴτʼ ἆσσον ὠκεῖς καὶ παραστάντες τάφῳ
sections.translation: hurry closer , and when you have reached the tomb , enter the opening where the stones of the mound have been torn away , up to the cell ’ s very mouth . See if it is Haemon ’ s voice that I recognize , or if I am cheated by the gods .

sections.label: Section 1219
sections.original: τάδʼ ἐξ ἀθύμου δεσπότου κελευσμάτων
sections.translation: This search , at our desperate master ’ s word ,

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: ἠθροῦμεν · ἐν δὲ λοισθίῳ τυμβεύματι
sections.translation: we went to make , and in the furthest part of the tomb we saw her hanging by the neck , fastened by a halter of fine linen threads , while he was embracing her with arms thrown around her waist , bewailing the loss of his bride to the spirits below , as well as his father ’ s deeds , and his grief-filled marriage .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: καὶ πατρὸς ἔργα καὶ τὸ δύστηνον λέχος .
sections.translation: But his father , when he saw him , cried aloud with a dreadful cry and went in and called to him with a voice of wailing : Ah , unhappy boy , what have you done ! What plan have you seized on ? By what misfortune have you lost your reason ?

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ἔξελθε , τέκνον , ἱκέσιός σε λίσσομαι .
sections.translation: Come out , my son , I pray you , I beg you ! But the boy glared at him with savage eyes , spat in his face , and without a word in response drew his twin-edged sword . As his father rushed out in flight , he missed his aim . Then the ill-fated boy was enraged with himself

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: αὑτῷ χολωθείς , ὥσπερ εἶχʼ , ἐπενταθεὶς
sections.translation: and straightway stretched himself over his sword and drove it , half its length , into his side . Still conscious , he clasped the maiden in his faint embrace , and , as he gasped , he shot onto her pale cheek a swift stream of oozing blood .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: κεῖται δὲ νεκρὸς περὶ νεκρῷ , τὰ νυμφικὰ
sections.translation: Corpse enfolding corpse he lay , having won his marriage rites , poor boy , not here , but in Hades ’ palace , and having shown to mankind by how much the failure to reason wisely is the most severe of all afflictions assigned to man . Eurydice departs into the house .

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: τί τοῦτʼ ἂν εἰκάσειας ; ἡ γυνὴ πάλιν
sections.translation: What would you infer from this ? The lady

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: φρούδη , πρὶν εἰπεῖν ἐσθλὸν ἢ κακὸν λόγον .
sections.translation: has turned back and gone without a word , either for good or for evil .

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: καὐτὸς τεθάμβηκʼ · ἐλπίσιν δὲ βόσκομαι
sections.translation: I , too , am startled . Still I am nourished by the hope that at the grave news of her son she thinks it unworthy to make her laments before the city , but in the shelter of her home will set her handmaids to mourn the house ’ s grief .

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: γνώμης γὰρ οὐκ ἄπειρος , ὥσθʼ ἁμαρτάνειν .
sections.translation: For she is not unhabituated to discretion , that she should err .

sections.label: Section 1251
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἐμοὶ δʼ οὖν ἥ τʼ ἄγαν σιγὴ βαρὺ
sections.translation: I do not know . But to me , in any case , a silence too strict seems to promise trouble just as much as a fruitless abundance of weeping .

sections.label: Section 1253
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰσόμεσθα , μή τι καὶ κατάσχετον
sections.translation: I will find out whether she is not , in fact , hiding some repressed plan in the darkness of her passionate heart .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: δόμους παραστείχοντες · εὖ γὰρ οὖν λέγεις .
sections.translation: I will go in , since you are right — in an excess of silence , too , there may be trouble . Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 1257
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδʼ ἄναξ αὐτὸς ἐφήκει
sections.translation: Look , here is the King himself approaching , his hands grasping a monument plainly signing that his — if we may say it — and no one else ’ s ,

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: ἄτην , ἀλλʼ αὐτὸς ἁμαρτών .
sections.translation: was the madness of this error .

sections.label: Section 1.1261
sections.original: ἰὼ
sections.translation: Ah , the blunders of an unthinking mind , blunders of rigidity , yielding death ! Oh , you witnesses of the killers and the killed , both of one family !
